 {"id":"ntm-ozcj.5","title":"CASS Context Injection: Provide relevant past context when spawning agents","description":"Automatically inject relevant past context when spawning new agents, reducing duplicate problem-solving.

## Overview
When `ntm spawn` or `ntm add` creates a new agent pane, query CASS for relevant past sessions based on:
- Current project/workspace
- Prompt content (if provided)
- Recent activity in the session

## Implementation

### Spawn with Context
```bash
# Auto-context mode (default when CASS available)
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --prompt="implement OAuth"
# CASS query: workspace:/path/to/myproject AND "OAuth"
# Injects: "Previous OAuth work in this project: [summary]"

# Explicit context query
ntm spawn myproject --cc=1 --cass-context="authentication patterns"
# Injects relevant past sessions about auth

# Disable context
ntm spawn myproject --cc=1 --no-cass-context
```

### Context Injection Format
```
## Relevant Past Sessions

This project has prior work on similar topics:

1. **OAuth Implementation** (3 days ago, claude_code)
   - Used passport.js with OAuth2 strategy
   - JWT tokens stored in Redis
   - Key file: src/auth/oauth.ts
   
2. **Session Management** (1 week ago, codex)
   - Express sessions with Redis store
   - CORS configuration for cookies
   
Consider reviewing these before re-implementing.
```

### Flags
- --cass-context <query>: Explicit context query
- --no-cass-context: Disable auto-context
- --cass-context-limit <n>: Max sessions to include (default 3)
- --cass-context-days <n>: Look back N days (default 30)

### Robot Mode
```bash
ntm --robot-spawn-context myproject --prompt="implement OAuth"
# Returns JSON with suggested context
{
  "workspace": "/path/to/myproject",
  "prompt": "implement OAuth",
  "relevant_sessions": [...],
  "suggested_injection": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawn queries CASS for relevant context
- [ ] Context injected into agent prompt
- [ ] Can disable with --no-cass-context
- [ ] Works with spawn, add, send commands
- [ ] Graceful when CASS unavailable
- [ ] Robot mode returns context JSON","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.45423-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.45423-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.455375-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.457942-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:17.458831-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-ozcj.6","title":"CASS Duplicate Detection: Warn before solving already-solved problems","description":"Detect and warn when an agent is about to work on something that was already solved in a past session.

## Overview
When sending prompts to agents, check CASS for similar past work and optionally:
- Show warning to user
- Inject "check this first" context to agent
- Link to past session for reference

## Implementation

### Send with Duplicate Check
```bash
ntm send myproject --all "fix the authentication bug"
# CASS check: "fix authentication bug" in recent sessions
# If found:
⚠ Similar work found in past sessions:
  1. "Debug auth flow" (2 days ago, claude_code)
     Preview: Fixed JWT validation issue in middleware
     
  Continue anyway? [y/N/view]
  - y: Send as-is
  - N: Cancel
  - view: Open past session in 
```

### Flags
- --cass-check: Force duplicate check (default: on)
- --no-cass-check: Skip duplicate check
- --cass-similarity <n>: Similarity threshold (0-100, default 70)
- --cass-check-days <n>: Look back N days (default 7)

### Robot Mode
```bash
ntm --robot-check-duplicates myproject --msg="fix auth bug"
{
  "query": "fix auth bug",
  "duplicates_found": true,
  "similar_sessions": [
    {
      "source_path": "/path/to/session.jsonl",
      "title": "Debug auth flow",
      "similarity": 0.85,
      "agent": "claude_code",
      "when": "2 days ago",
      "preview": "Fixed JWT validation..."
    }
  ],
  "recommendation": "review_before_proceeding"
}
```

### Integration Points
- ntm send: Check before broadcasting
- ntm prompt (if added): Check before sending
- TUI: Show warning in palette before confirming send

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Duplicate check on send command
- [ ] User can view, skip, or proceed
- [ ] Configurable similarity threshold
- [ ] Robot mode returns check results
- [ ] Works in TUI palette
- [ ] Fast (<200ms) check time","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:37.267835-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:37.267835-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.6","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:37.269444-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.6","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:37.270998-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-ozcj.7","title":"CASS + MCP Agent Mail: Cross-agent knowledge sharing","description":"Integrate CASS with MCP Agent Mail for intelligent cross-agent coordination.

## Overview
Enable agents to check CASS before starting work and share findings via Agent Mail, creating a knowledge-sharing loop.

## Use Cases

### Before Starting Work
When agent claims a task:
1. Query CASS for similar past work
2. If found, include in agent context
3. Agent can decide to reuse vs. re-implement

### After Completing Work
When agent finishes:
1. New session auto-indexed by CASS
2. Summary posted to Agent Mail thread
3. Other agents can reference it

### Cross-Project Knowledge
When working on similar problems across projects:
1. Query CASS without workspace filter
2. Find solutions from other projects
3. Adapt patterns (not copy code)

## Implementation

### Agent Mail Integration
```go
// Before claiming task
func (c *Coordinator) CheckCASSBeforeWork(task string) (*CASSContext, error) {
    results, err := c.cass.Search(task, &SearchOptions{
        Limit: 5,
        Since: "30d",
    })
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    if len(results.Hits) > 0 {
        return &CASSContext{
            RelevantSessions: results.Hits,
            Suggestion: "Review these before re-implementing",
        }, nil
    }
    return nil, nil
}

// After completing work
func (c *Coordinator) NotifyCompletionWithCASSRef(taskID, sessionPath string) error {
    // Post to Agent Mail with CASS reference
    return c.mail.SendMessage(Message{
        Subject: fmt.Sprintf("Completed: %s", taskID),
        Body: fmt.Sprintf("Work completed. Session indexed in CASS: %s", sessionPath),
        ThreadID: taskID,
    })
}
```

### Workflow Example
```
1. Agent claims beads task "implement rate limiting"
2. NTM queries CASS: "rate limiting" in last 30 days
3. Found: Session from 2 weeks ago implementing rate limiting
4. Agent receives context: "Past implementation used token bucket..."
5. Agent adapts pattern, completes faster
6. Posts to Agent Mail: "Completed. Improved on past approach."
7. New session indexed, available for future agents
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agents receive CASS context when claiming tasks
- [ ] Completed work notified via Agent Mail
- [ ] Cross-project search works
- [ ] Integration with beads workflow
- [ ] Configurable via config file","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:59.271289-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:59.271289-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.7","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:59.272709-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.7","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:59.273997-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.7","depends_on_id":"ntm-vum","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:31:59.275255-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"ntm-ozcj.8","title":"CASS Configuration: Add config options for CASS integration","description":"Add configuration options for CASS integration.

## Config Schema

Add to ~/.config/ntm/config.toml:

```toml
[cass]
# Master switch - disable all CASS features
enabled = true

# Show installation hints when CASS not found
show_install_hints = true

# Path to cass binary (auto-detect from PATH if not set)
binary_path = ""

# Timeout for CASS operations (seconds)
timeout = 30

# Context injection settings
[cass.context]
enabled = true          # Auto-inject context when spawning
max_sessions = 3        # Max past sessions to include
lookback_days = 30      # How far back to search
max_tokens = 2000       # Token budget for context

# Duplicate detection settings
[cass.duplicates]
enabled = true          # Check for duplicates before sending
similarity_threshold = 0.7  # 0-1, higher = stricter
lookback_days = 7       # How far back to check
prompt_on_match = true  # Ask user before proceeding

# Search defaults
[cass.search]
default_limit = 10
default_fields = "summary"
include_meta = true

# TUI settings
[cass.tui]
show_activity_sparkline = true
show_status_indicator = true
```

## Implementation

### Config Structure
```go
type CASSConfig struct {
    Enabled          bool   `toml:"enabled"`
    ShowInstallHints bool   `toml:"show_install_hints"`
    BinaryPath       string `toml:"binary_path"`
    Timeout          int    `toml:"timeout"`
    
    Context    CASSContextConfig    `toml:"context"`
    Duplicates CASSDuplicateConfig  `toml:"duplicates"`
    Search     CASSSearchConfig     `toml:"search"`
    TUI        CASSTUIConfig        `toml:"tui"`
}
```

### Defaults
All features enabled by default for good out-of-box experience.
Users can disable individual features as needed.

### Environment Variables
```bash
NTM_CASS_ENABLED=false       # Override config
NTM_CASS_TIMEOUT=60          # Override timeout
NTM_CASS_BINARY=/path/to/cass  # Override binary path
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config schema defined
- [ ] Defaults are user-friendly
- [ ] Environment variable overrides work
- [ ] Config validation on load
- [ ] Changes take effect without restart (where possible)
- [ ] ntm config show includes CASS section","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:22.932371-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:22.932371-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.8","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:22.933068-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.8","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:22.935128-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-pb3","title":"Implement --robot-interrupt for priority course correction","description":"Interrupt an agent with high-priority instruction.

## Rationale
Sometimes I need to redirect an agent urgently - stop what you're doing, do this instead. Regular send just queues; interrupt needs to actually break the agent's current flow.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-interrupt myproject --pane=0.1 --msg="STOP: critical bug found, fix X first"
ntm --robot-interrupt myproject --all --msg="Halt all work, sync for team discussion"
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "interrupted": ["0.1"],
  "method": "ctrl_c_then_send",
  "previous_states": {
    "0.1": {"state": "active", "last_output": "..."}
  },
  "message_sent": true,
  "ready_for_input": true
}
```

## Interrupt Methods
1. Send Ctrl+C to break current operation
2. Wait for prompt/ready state
3. Send the interrupt message

## Options
- --force: Send Ctrl+C even if agent appears idle
- --no-wait: Don't wait for ready state
- --preserve-context: Try to save agent's context before interrupting

## Safety Considerations
- Log interrupted state for potential resume
- Avoid interrupting mid-file-write
- Rate limit to prevent interrupt storms","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:24.290441-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:24.290441-05:00"}
